content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes. The root of the tree is node 111.

Find the height of the tree.

The height of the tree is defined as the number of nodes along the longest path from the root node down to a leaf node.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of nodes.
The next (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge **from** uiu_iui​ **to** viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the height of the tree
constraints: ui≠viu_i \neq v_iui​=vi​ for each 1≤i<N1 \leq i \lt N1≤i<N.
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One of the longest paths from root node to leaf node is 1→21\rightarrow 21→2. Since there are 222 nodes in the path, the height of the tree is 222.
Test case 222: The longest path from root node to leaf node is 1→2→41\rightarrow 2\rightarrow 41→2→4. Since there are 333 nodes in the path, the height of the tree is 333.
Test case 333: One of the longest paths from root node to leaf node is 1→3→61\rightarrow 3\rightarrow 61→3→6. Since there are 333 nodes in the path, the height of the tree is 333.
